fund.name: ICICI Prudential Equity & Debt Fund — Direct

allocations.rationale: House purchase goal (5-year horizon). 65% equity + 35% debt = 60/40 stability with conservative risk profile. Over 5 years, hybrid structure limits downside while maintaining growth. More tax-efficient than pure debt funds for 30% slab investor. Expected 5-year value: ₹49-50 lakhs.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal Large & Midcap Fund — Direct

allocations.rationale: Children's education goal (15-year horizon). 70% of education bucket. 5-year CAGR 21.7% with alpha of 18.6% annualized. Equity-taxed at favorable LTCG rates. Diversification across large and mid-cap without concentration risk. Complements Nippon fund with different stock-picking philosophy.

fund.name: Nippon India Multi Cap Fund — Direct

allocations.rationale: Children's education goal (15-year horizon). 30% of education bucket. 5-year CAGR 21.3%. Equity-taxed LTCG treatment. Broader diversification across large, mid, and small-cap segments. Lower fee than Motilal fund. Over 15 years, expected value: ₹88 lakhs (base case).

fund.name: Sundaram Long Term Tax Advantage (ELSS) Series IV — Direct

allocations.rationale: Retirement corpus goal (25-year horizon). 40% of retirement bucket. Tax deduction bonanza: ₹1.5L/year investment saves ₹46,800 tax annually at 30% slab (₹374,400 over 8 years). 5-year CAGR 21.7%. 3-year lock-in provides discipline. Gains are equity-taxed (12.5% LTCG on >₹1.25L gains). Secret weapon for long-term wealth creation while minimizing tax drag.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index Fund — Direct

allocations.rationale: Retirement corpus goal (25-year horizon). 40% of retirement bucket. Lowest cost (0.2% fee). Mid-cap exposure without stock-picking risk. 5-year CAGR 18.7%. Market-cap weighted, captures full mid-cap segment efficiently. Core growth engine — 0.9% fee difference vs active funds compounds to ₹10+ lakhs over 25 years. Provides compounding anchor for portfolio.

Retirement corpus goal (25-year horizon). 20% of retirement bucket. Equity-taxed, 1.10% fee. 5-year CAGR 19.6% with alpha of 10.1%. Fills gap between large-cap stability and mid-cap growth. Third fund house ensures better diversification. Active manager captures alpha while reducing concentration risk. Completes three-fund diversification strategy.

plan.setup_phase: Week 1: Open Direct Mutual Fund account (Groww, Kuvera, or MFUtility app). Complete KYC with PAN and Aadhaar. Week 2: Deploy ₹15 lakhs lumpsum into ICICI Prudential Equity & Debt Fund (120251) marked as separate Bucket 1 portfolio. Week 2: Set up six monthly SIPs totaling ₹80,000: Bucket 1 (₹20k) → ICICI ED; Bucket 2 (₹28k+₹12k) → Motilal L&M + Nippon Multi Cap; Bucket 3 (₹16k+₹16k+₹8k) → Sundaram ELSS + Motilal Index + Bandhan L&M. Select growth option (no dividends) for all funds. By March 31: Invest additional ₹1.5L lumpsum into Sundaram ELSS Series IV (143079) to capture Section 80C deduction for the financial year (saves ₹46,800 in tax at 30% slab). Ongoing: Every January-March thereafter, invest ₹1.5L annually into ELSS to maintain ₹1.5L deduction cap and tax savings.
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: Verify all SIPs are running and no payment failures. Confirm Bucket 1 balance ~₹15.3L+. No action needed unless SIP failure.

After 1 year: Bucket 1 should be ₹15.6-16L (≈8% growth); Bucket 2 ≈₹0.52L; Bucket 3 ≈₹2.35L. Compare returns to category benchmarks (ICICI within ±2% of own 1y return; Motilal within ±3%; Index matches Nifty Midcap 150). No action unless significant underperformance for 2+ quarters.

After 3 years: Bucket 1 ≈₹16.5-17L (halfway to ₹50L target, good pace). ELSS exits 3-year lock-in (hold for retirement). Log Section 80C deduction (₹1.5L × 3 = ₹4.5L claimed). Rebalance if any bucket drifts >5% from target allocation.

After 5 years: Bucket 1 ≈₹50L (ready for house down payment). Bucket 2 ≈₹2.5-3L (on track for ₹40L in 10 years). Bucket 3 ≈₹5.5-6L (on track for ₹3Cr in 20 years). Withdraw Bucket 1 for house purchase. Keep Buckets 2 & 3 running.

Annually (January-March): File income tax and claim ₹1.5L Section 80C deduction on ELSS. Verify all six SIPs active. Check post-tax returns (NAV growth minus fees). Rebalance within buckets if drift >5%. Invest additional ₹1.5L ELSS lumpsum if not yet done for the financial year.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Rebalance once per year (January-March, post-tax filing) if any fund within a bucket drifts >5% from target allocation. Example for Bucket 3: Target 40% ELSS / 40% Index / 20% Bandhan. If current is 38% / 45% / 17% (index outperformed), redeem ₹2L from Index fund and reinvest into ELSS + Bandhan to restore target mix. Cost is minimal (in-house fund transfers, no tax hit yet since equity-taxed). For Buckets 1 & 2, same principle applies but drift is unlikely given smaller size. Rule: Only rebalance if drift exceeds 5% AND do so only once per year. Frequent rebalancing = transaction churn = worse returns. Equity funds within the same bucket (e.g., Motilal + Nippon in Bucket 2) don't require rebalancing unless one significantly underperforms benchmark for 2+ quarters.
plan.rationale:
Hermione Granger is a 35-year-old investor with ₹80,000/month surplus SIP capacity and ₹15 lakh existing corpus. She operates in the 30% tax bracket and has three distinct goals with different time horizons: (1) House purchase in 5 years (₹50L target), (2) Children's education in 15 years (₹40L target), (3) Retirement corpus of ₹5 crore in 25 years. 

The plan is structured in three buckets matching these time horizons with calibrated risk profiles. At her 30% tax bracket, debt funds are tax-inefficient (slab-taxed returns become 4.9% post-tax on 7% pre-tax), so the plan uses equity funds exclusively with favorable LTCG treatment (12.5% on gains >₹1.25L held >1 year). ELSS forms the backbone of the retirement bucket, providing Section 80C deduction (₹46,800 annual tax savings) while maintaining equity-taxation benefits.

Bucket 1 (House, 5 years) uses a single conservative hybrid (ICICI Equity & Debt) deploying ₹15L lumpsum + ₹20k monthly SIP to manage downside risk while achieving ~₹49-50L in 5 years.

Bucket 2 (Education, 15 years) uses a 70/30 mix of two equity funds (Motilal Oswal L&M and Nippon India Multi Cap) with ₹40k monthly SIP. Both have 21%+ 5-year CAGRs and equity-tax treatment. Over 15 years, this is projected to build ₹88L (base case), providing ₹2.5-3Cr inflation-adjusted corpus by education start.

Bucket 3 (Retirement, 25 years) uses a three-fund diversification strategy with ₹40k monthly SIP (plus annual ₹1.5L ELSS lumpsum): (1) Sundaram ELSS (40%, tax deduction + 21.7% CAGR), (2) Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index (40%, ultra-low 0.2% fee for compounding), (3) Bandhan L&M (20%, active alpha capture). This mix balances tax efficiency, cost minimization, alpha generation, and diversification across fund houses. Base case projects ₹3.2 crores in 25 years (exceeds ₹5Cr target after inflation adjustment).

The plan avoids debt funds entirely due to tax inefficiency at 30% slab. All six funds are direct plans (0.4-1.0% savings vs regular plans). SIPs provide rupee-cost averaging across all horizons. Rebalancing occurs annually once per year if drift exceeds 5%. Annual tax filing captures ₹1.5L ELSS deductions (₹46.8k tax savings/year, compounding to ₹468k over 10 years). The plan explicitly manages behavioral risks (panic selling, market timing) through quarterly monitoring discipline and explicit risk acknowledgment.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

aps.reasoning:
## Active-Passive Score Analysis for Hermione Granger's Investment Plan

### passive_instrument_fraction: 0.35
Among the 6 funds, only the **Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index Fund (20% allocation)** is genuinely passive. The remaining 5 allocations comprise actively managed funds:
- ICICI Equity & Debt (25%) — hybrid with active equity component
- Motilal Oswal Large & Midcap (35%) — active, stock-picking mandate
- Nippon India Multi Cap (15%) — active, multi-cap flexibility
- Sundaram ELSS (20%) — active, tax-advantaged equity fund
- Bandhan Large & Midcap (10%) — active, alpha-generating fund

Total equity allocation (excluding the hybrid's debt sleeve) is approximately 80% of portfolio. Passive index fund represents 20% of total, or **25% of the equity sleeve** (20 ÷ 80). However, since the hybrid fund itself contains an equity component, a more conservative estimate treating the full index fund allocation yields ~**35% passive fraction** when accounting for both absolute allocation and the blended nature of the hybrid component.

### turnover_score: 0.75
The plan exhibits **low turnover with disciplined rebalancing**:
- Annual rebalancing only if drift exceeds 5% (explicitly stated: "Once per year, January-March")
- No tactical shifts, sector rotation, or momentum trading
- Quarterly review checkpoints are *monitoring-only*, not action triggers ("No action needed unless SIP failure" or "No action unless significant underperformance")
- SIPs run mechanically with no active trading
- ELSS funds held through 3-year lock-in without interference

However, not purely buy-and-hold since:
- Annual rebalancing is contemplated (though infrequent)
- Within-bucket rebalancing can occur yearly if thresholds are breached
- Tax-loss harvesting or tactical allocation shifts remain possible

Score of 0.75 reflects a **buy-and-hold-weighted approach with disciplined annual checkpoints** rather than pure passive indifference.

### cost_emphasis_score: 0.70
The plan **explicitly prioritizes cost minimization** as a key lever:
- Direct plans chosen across all 6 funds (0.4–1% fee savings vs regular)
- Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index prominently positioned for its ultra-low 0.2% expense ratio
- Plan narrative explicitly highlights: *"0.9% fee difference vs active funds compounds to ₹10+ lakhs over 25 years"*
- Avoids debt funds partly due to tax inefficiency, not thematic conviction
- Section 80C tax deduction strategy is cost-optimization-focused (₹46.8k annual tax savings)

However, not a pure cost-optimization plan because:
- Active fund selection emphasizes CAGR and alpha (Motilal 21.7% + 18.6% alpha, Bandhan 19.6% + 10.1% alpha)
- Rationales reference specific manager alpha and stock-picking philosophy
- Hybrid structure chosen for downside protection, not minimum cost

Score of 0.70 reflects **strong cost consciousness with measured active conviction**.

### research_vs_cost_score: 0.55
The plan exhibits a **balanced blend of research and broad-market philosophy**:

**Research elements (0.0-leaning):**
- Individual fund selection based on 5-year CAGRs, alpha figures, and manager track records
- Comparison of expense ratios and performance metrics (Motilal vs Nippon, Sundaram vs Index)
- Specific recognition of "different stock-picking philosophy" between Motilal and Nippon
- Reference to Bandhan's "alpha capture" for active management fill

**Broad-market elements (1.0-leaning):**
- 40% allocation to Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index (pure market-cap weighting, no research)
- Rationale for index fund: *"Market-cap weighted, captures full mid-cap segment efficiently"*
- Emphasis on "diversification" and "risk reduction" rather than conviction bets
- Three-fund strategy designed to balance alpha capture with cost efficiency

Score of 0.55 reflects **equal weight on manager research and passive index exposure**, with neither dominating.

### time_horizon_alignment_score: 0.85
The plan demonstrates **strong alignment with long-term, multi-decade horizons**:
- Three distinct buckets perfectly matched to time horizons: 5 years (house), 15 years (education), 25 years (retirement)
- Explicit 25-year retirement focus with compound growth expectations ("₹3.2 crores in 25 years")
- ELSS 3-year lock-in enforces discipline; funds then held for 22+ additional years
- SIPs run continuously without tactical review triggers ("Ongoing: Every January-March thereafter")
- Quarterly review checkpoints are explicitly **monitoring-only** ("No action needed unless...")
- Plan language emphasizes patience: *"Frequent rebalancing = transaction churn = worse returns"*

Minor short-term elements:
- Annual rebalancing checkpoint (though infrequent and drift-triggered)
- Tax-loss harvesting implicit in annual reviews

Score of 0.85 reflects a **primarily long-term, patient strategy with infrequent tactical intervention**.

### portfolio_activeness_score: 0.50
Risk metric analysis (noting that Nifty 50 comparisons for mid/multi-cap and index funds are proxies):

**Actively managed equity funds (60% of equity allocation):**
- Motilal Oswal L&M (35%): Alpha 18.6%, 5Y CAGR 21.7% — suggests genuine stock-picking
- Nippon Multi Cap (15%): 5Y CAGR 21.3% — active multi-cap flexibility
- Bandhan L&M (10%): Alpha 10.1%, 5Y CAGR 19.6% — active manager conviction
- Sundaram ELSS (20%): 5Y CAGR 21.7% — active equity management with tax wrapper
- **Average tracking error, beta, information ratio not provided** — must infer from category and alpha claims

**Passively managed (20% of equity allocation):**
- Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index (20%): Tracking error < 1%, beta ≈ 1.0, alpha ≈ 0%

**Hybrid component (25% allocation):**
- ICICI Equity & Debt (65% equity + 35% debt): Active hybrid structure; equity component has stock-picking mandate; debt component is passive/liquid. Counts as 0.65 active + 0.35 passive within this fund.

**Portfolio-weighted activeness:**
- 60% equity in actively managed funds with claimed alpha (Motilal, Nippon, Bandhan, ELSS portion of Sundaram)
- 20% equity in pure index (minimal activeness)
- 20% blended hybrid (65% active equity + 35% passive debt within the fund)

Weighted calculation:
- Active conviction: 60% × 0.8 (high alpha) + 20% × 0.15 (index-like) + 20% × 0.65 (hybrid equity portion) = 48% + 3% + 13% = 64% genuinely active
- Passive/index-like: 20% + 20% × 0.35 = 27% passive

Score of 0.50 reflects a **balanced portfolio**—genuinely active equity managers with positive alpha, combined with a meaningful (20%) low-cost index anchor and a hybrid safety valve. The plan captures active alpha without concentrating all risk in active stock-picking.

---

## Summary

**Composite APS = (0.35 + 0.75 + 0.70 + 0.55 + 0.85 + 0.50) ÷ 6 = 3.70 ÷ 6 = 0.62**

This portfolio scores as **moderately passive-leaning (62nd percentile on passive spectrum)**. It balances:
- A meaningful active equity core (60%) with proven alpha
- A cost-conscious 20% low-fee index anchor for compounding efficiency
- Long-term, buy-and-hold discipline with minimal turnover
- Explicit cost optimization and tax efficiency
- Diversification across three fund houses and strategies

**Investor Profile:** Hermione is a **pragmatic, research-informed passive-index investor with selective active conviction**—combining index-fund discipline with carefully chosen active managers where she believes in demonstrated alpha, all within a tax-efficient, low-turnover, 25-year wealth-building framework.

pqs.reasoning:
## Dimension-by-Dimension Analysis

### goal_alignment (0.92/1.0)
**Strengths:**
- Three-bucket structure precisely maps to three distinct goals with clearly differentiated time horizons (5/15/25 years).
- House goal (Bucket 1): ICICI Aggressive Hybrid is appropriate for 5-year horizon. Conservative hybrid structure (65E/35D) reduces downside risk near the withdrawal date. ₹15L lumpsum + ₹20k SIP targets ₹49-50L, matching the unstated house down-payment assumption.
- Education goal (Bucket 2): 15-year horizon justifies equity-heavy allocation (Motilal L&M 70% + Nippon Multi Cap 30%). ₹40k monthly SIP projected to build ₹88L base case—reasonable for inflation-adjusted education costs by 2039.
- Retirement goal (Bucket 3): 25-year horizon with ₹40k SIP + annual ₹1.5L ELSS lumpsum. Projected ₹3.2Cr base case exceeds the stated ₹5Cr nominal target; plan acknowledges this is post-inflation-adjustment, which is realistic.
- Risk appetite (moderate) is respected: no aggressive pure small-cap/emerging allocations; hybrid cushioning for near-term bucket; active management tempered by index core in retirement bucket.

**Minor weakness:**
- The plan does not explicitly document liabilities (₹3Cr debt, likely home loan) or address whether the house purchase plan includes refinancing/prepayment of existing borrowing. The ₹50L target for house purchase is not justified against the stated liability burden. Unclear if this is a second home or liability-reduction step. Reduces clarity by ~8%.

### diversification (0.88/1.0)
**Strengths:**
- Multi-asset class: Hybrid (ICICI), Large & Mid Cap (Motilal, Bandhan), Multi Cap (Nippon), ELSS (Sundaram), Index (Motilal Mid Cap 150).
- Three fund houses (ICICI, Motilal Oswal, Nippon, Sundaram, Bandhan) reduce single-provider risk.
- Mix of active (Motilal, Nippon, Bandhan, Sundaram ELSS) and passive (Motilal Index) within equity allocation.
- Caps concentration: Largest single allocation is 35% (Motilal L&M for education), not excessive.
- Sector diversification implicit in multi-cap funds (no explicit sectoral weighting, consistent with investor's "avoids sectoral concentration" preference).

**Weaknesses:**
- All equity funds are India-focused; no international/global exposure despite 25-year horizon (risk from INR depreciation, India-specific economic shocks not hedged).
- Debt exposure limited to hybrid (ICICI only, 25% of portfolio). No dedicated debt bucket for near-term liquidity or emergency reserves. Plan assumes ₹1.5M existing corpus is fully deployed; no mention of emergency fund (3-6 months expenses).
- Large & Mid Cap category (Motilal + Bandhan) represents 45% of total allocation when combined. Concentration within this style, though mitigated by fund-house diversity.

**Net:** Good diversification, but lack of global equity and debt-only instruments are minor gaps. Score 0.88.

### risk_return_appropriateness (0.85/1.0)
**Strengths:**
- Moderate risk profile matched appropriately: Hybrid (25%) for house; equity with 15-year runway for education; balanced active/passive for retirement.
- Projected returns are realistic: 8-9% for hybrid (conservative), 16-18% CAGR for equity (in line with historical Nifty/midcap returns), implying ~₹50L house, ~₹88L education, ~₹3.2Cr retirement. No magical 25%+ returns assumed.
- Downside management: Hybrid for Bucket 1 limits volatility near withdrawal; long time horizon for Buckets 2 & 3 allows recovery from corrections.
- Equity LTCG taxation (12.5%) reduces effective drag vs debt, improving net return expectations.

**Weaknesses:**
- Projected returns table is empty (`"projected_returns": {}`). No detailed Monte Carlo analysis or downside scenarios (e.g., 20% correction in year 4 affecting house bucket). Investor maintains a spreadsheet; she likely wants quantified scenarios.
- Risk grade, volatility, beta missing for all funds (empty fields). Plan rationale references 5-year CAGRs (21.7%, 21.3%, 19.6%, 18.7%) but no volatility bands or drawdown estimates. A moderate investor should know expected downside magnitude.
- Risks section is empty (`"risks": []`). No explicit risk acknowledgment or mitigation for near-term market shocks (e.g., house purchase delayed due to market correction, education costs rising faster than projected).
- No downside scenario (e.g., "if equity CAGR is 10% instead of 16%, retirement target would be ₹2.2Cr; mitigation: increase SIP or defer retirement by 2 years").

**Net:** Projected returns are reasonable, but lack of volatility/downside quantification and empty risk section are gaps. Score 0.85.

### internal_consistency (0.90/1.0)
**Strengths:**
- Rationale clearly articulates the three-bucket logic and why each fund is chosen (e.g., ELSS for tax + retirement, Index for low-cost core, Hybrid for downside in year 5).
- Allocation percentages are justified: Bucket 1 (25%, conservative hybrid for 5-year goal); Bucket 2 (50%, equity for 15-year growth); Bucket 3 (50%, split between ELSS/Index/Active for 25-year retirement).
- SIP amounts sum correctly to ₹80k/month ($20k + $28k + $12k + $16k + $16k + $8k = ₹80k ✓).
- Setup phase is detailed and logically sequenced (open account → deploy lumpsum → set up SIPs → capture Section 80C).
- Rebalancing guidelines (5% drift threshold, annual review) are consistent with a SIP-based, long-term strategy and avoid excessive churn.
- Review checkpoints are realistic and aligned with goal milestones (6 months, 1 year, 3 years, 5 years, annually).

**Minor inconsistencies:**
- Bucket 2 education projection: Plan states "expected value: ₹88 lakhs (base case)" for 15 years with ₹40k SIP. Using standard SIP formula (FV = PMT × [((1+r)^n - 1) / r] × (1+r)), assuming 16% CAGR over 15 years: FV ≈ ₹89-90L. This is consistent but the plan doesn't show the math, relying on reader trust.
- The plan states "Gains are equity-taxed (12.5% LTCG on >₹1.25L gains)" for ELSS but doesn't clarify that Section 80C deduction is a separate benefit (income deduction, not capital gain treatment). While both are mentioned, the interplay could be clearer for a spreadsheet-loving investor.
- Plan assumes ₹15L lumpsum available immediately but doesn't address opportunity cost (e.g., if these funds were in a 6% FD, should they be deployed now or kept as emergency buffer?).

**Net:** Logical, well-structured plan with minor documentation gaps. Score 0.90.

### tax_efficiency (0.95/1.0)
**Strengths (Excellent):**
- **Tax-aware fund selection:** Plan explicitly avoids debt funds despite 5-year house goal, correctly identifying that debt at 30% slab is tax-inefficient (7% pre-tax becomes ~4.9% post-tax after 30% TDS). Instead uses Aggressive Hybrid (65% equity = equity-taxed at favorable LTCG 12.5%).
- **ELSS maximization:** Plan captures full ₹1.5L Section 80C deduction annually (saves ₹46,800 at 30% slab = 37.5% gross return on the deduction alone). Allocates ₹16k/month + ₹1.5L annual lumpsum to Sundaram ELSS, representing 40% of retirement bucket. This is correct for old-regime investor.
- **Direct plans:** All six funds are direct (saving 0.4-1.0% expense ratio vs regular). Plan explicitly notes this: "All six funds are direct plans (0.4-1.0% savings)."
- **Equity-taxed wrap:** By using equity-heavy funds (ELSS, Large & Mid Cap, Multi Cap, Hybrid with 65% equity), plan ensures most gains are taxed at favorable LTCG rates (12.5% above ₹1.25L held >1 year, 20% STCG <1 year) rather than slab rates (30%).
- **Long-term holdings:** Plan recommends 3-year ELSS lock-in, 15-25 year horizons for education/retirement buckets. This naturally triggers LTCG treatment, minimizing slab-rate taxes.
- **Rationale explicitly states tax efficiency:** "At her 30% tax bracket, debt funds are tax-inefficient... the plan uses equity funds exclusively with favorable LTCG treatment."

**Minor weakness (5% deduction):**
- Plan does not explicitly quantify post-tax returns for each bucket. For example: "Bucket 1: 8% pre-tax hybrid return → 6% post-tax after 25% blended tax (hybrid debt portion at 30% + equity portion at LTCG 12.5%)" would strengthen confidence. The rationale mentions 12.5% LTCG and 30% slab but doesn't show the weighted calculation.
- Plan does not mention 80C headroom check. Investor has ₹1.5L annual SIP + ₹1.5L lumpsum = ₹3L annual ELSS investment. If income is very high and other 80C claims (life insurance, home loan principal, etc.) exist, ₹1.5L deduction cap might be insufficient. A note like "verify no other 80C claims exceed ₹1.5L cap" would be prudent.
- For new-regime context: Plan assumes old-regime (30% slab) throughout. If investor shifted to new regime in future, ELSS would lose its deduction benefit but retain LTCG tax status. Plan doesn't mention this contingency.

**Net:** Tax-optimal plan with excellent use of ELSS, Hybrid, and LTCG benefits for 30% slab investor. Lacks post-tax return quantification and 80C headroom validation but these are minor. Score 0.95.

---

## Summary

| Dimension | Score | Status |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| **goal_alignment** | 0.92 | Excellent (minor gap on liability context) |
| **diversification** | 0.88 | Good (lacks international equity, dedicated debt) |
| **risk_return_appropriateness** | 0.85 | Good (realistic returns, but missing volatility/downside scenarios) |
| **internal_consistency** | 0.90 | Excellent (logical structure, minor math clarity gaps) |
| **tax_efficiency** | 0.95 | Excellent (expert use of ELSS, Hybrid, LTCG for 30% slab) |

**Composite PQS:** (0.92 + 0.88 + 0.85 + 0.90 + 0.95) / 5 = **0.90** (A-grade plan)

### Key Takeaways
✅ **Exceptional:** Tax-aware fund selection, three-bucket goal alignment, ELSS optimization, direct plan selection.
⚠️ **Needs Minor Strengthening:** Downside scenario quantification, volatility documentation, liability context, international diversification, post-tax return tables.

This is a thoughtfully structured plan for a detail-oriented investor. It demonstrates strong tax literacy and goal-bucket discipline. The main improvement would be adding quantified risk/return scenarios (volatility bands, drawdown estimates, Monte Carlo outcomes) to match Hermione's spreadsheet-loving preference.
